diligence_item: UMC internal customer overlap by country
why_it_matters: Commercial presence only matters where named UMC accounts or prospects require local coverage.
next_action: Map Fab 12i and mature-node customers to SEA manufacturing, EMS, packaging, and support footprints.
output_needed: Country-by-customer overlap map with account owner and estimated opportunity type.

why_it_matters: A hub requires UMC-specific FAE, quality, design-in, or escalation workload.
next_action: Interview account teams and identify workflows that cannot be handled from Singapore or Taiwan.
output_needed: Validated workflow list with volume, urgency, and customer owner.

why_it_matters: Management needs to know whether value comes from revenue, retention, support efficiency, cycle time, or option learning.
next_action: Define value mechanism by option family and country.
output_needed: Option-level financial logic memo with measurable benefit drivers.

diligence_item: Capex, opex, payback, and ROIC ranges
why_it_matters: Manufacturing expansion and partnership options need cost envelopes before deeper comparison.
next_action: Prepare light scenario ranges for office, hub, partnership, and manufacturing models.
output_needed: Scenario model with assumptions, sensitivities, and unresolved inputs.

diligence_item: Utility cost, reliability, power, and water availability
why_it_matters: Manufacturing feasibility depends on site-level power, water, redundancy, permitting, and reliability.
next_action: Collect site-level tariff, outage, power, water, and permitting data.
output_needed: Country/site utility fact pack with data gaps clearly marked.

why_it_matters: Generic engineering supply does not confirm FAE, product, quality, test, packaging, or process capability.
next_action: Define target roles and validate available talent pools by country.
output_needed: Role-by-country talent availability and training gap assessment.

diligence_item: Fab 12i P3 baseline constraints and extension needs
why_it_matters: Post-P3 options should be compared against actual customer, technology, capacity, and support constraints.
next_action: Summarize P3 ramp milestones, customer qualification status, and support bottlenecks.
output_needed: Fab 12i P3 baseline fact pack for option comparison.

why_it_matters: Partner-first logic requires named partners with capacity, quality, commercial, and IP fit.
next_action: Create partner screen across OSAT, test, substrate, materials, and logistics nodes.
output_needed: Partner shortlist with capability, commercial fit, risk, and validation status.

why_it_matters: A partner ecosystem matters only if Fab 12i customers can and will attach relevant package/test flows.
next_action: Estimate attach-rate by customer/product and identify qualification or IP constraints.

why_it_matters: Policy headlines are not project terms; incentives need eligibility, timing, conditions, and clawback details.
next_action: Request country-level incentive menus for each option type.
output_needed: Incentive comparison matrix by option, country, and eligibility condition.

diligence_item: Entity, tax, export-control, IP, and partner-structure review
why_it_matters: Operating setup and partner models can create tax, IP, customer-data, and compliance constraints.
next_action: Prepare issue list by option family and country.
output_needed: Legal and tax feasibility memo with open issues and required counsel review.

why_it_matters: Cost comparison requires semiconductor-specific roles rather than generic engineer salary proxies.
next_action: Collect salary bands for FAE, quality, test, packaging, process, product, and program roles.
output_needed: Function-level salary range and hiring competitiveness table.
